title: Aureline settings sync device-record
description: Boundary schema for the optional-sync device record. See docs/adr/0008-settings-definition-and-effective-configuration-resolver.md and docs/settings/sync_and_device_registry_seed.md. One record per device that ever participated in the settings optional-sync lane. The record names the device identity, user-chosen label, device class, os-family class, identity mode (ADR-0001), trust state, registration and last-seen timestamps, revocation lifecycle, revocation actor, typed capability posture, export-safe lineage cursor, and redaction class applied on every exportable surface. Raw device secrets never appear; the record references a device-secret class label (ADR-0007) and a broker alias only. The eventual settings crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary that the settings UI, CLI settings device command, audit stream, support-bundle exporter, migration center, and optional-sync transport read. Adding a new top-level field, device class, os-family class, revocation state, revocation reason, or capability-state kind is additive-minor and bumps settings_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

settings_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this sync device-record vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

device_id.description: Opaque stable device id minted by the resolver on first registration. Never a raw hostname, MAC address, serial, or user id. The support exporter and audit stream may quote this value; UI surfaces render the user-chosen device_label instead.

device_label.description: User-chosen friendly label for the device (for example, 'Home Mac mini', 'Dev laptop'). Redacted per ADR-0007 when the device record appears in a support bundle that crosses an org boundary.

device_class.description: Frozen device-class vocabulary. 'unknown' is reserved for records imported from legacy tools that did not declare a class; a normal registration MUST declare a concrete class.

os_family_class.description: Coarse os-family class used on export. The raw os version, kernel build, and hostname never cross the sync boundary; only the class travels. Adding a class is additive-minor and bumps settings_schema_version.

identity_mode.description: ADR-0001 identity mode at the moment of registration. A record whose identity_mode later changes mints a new device_id; the old record is carried through the revocation lifecycle rather than rewritten in place.

trust_state.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state observed on the device at last_seen_at. Informational only; sync never elevates trust.

revocation_state.description: Frozen revocation-state vocabulary. 'active' is the normal participating state. 'paused' is a user-initiated freeze: the device keeps its lineage cursor but neither pushes nor pulls. 'revoked' is a durable refusal: the device rejects all sync traffic and its scope bundles are never consumed; the record stays in the registry for audit. 'forgotten' is the terminal state: the device label and lineage cursor are purged modulo the retention window declared by the record_class_registry; device_id and revocation stamps remain so the audit stream stays complete.

revocation_reason.description: Frozen revocation-reason vocabulary. 'none' pairs with revocation_state=active. 'migration_superseded' fires when a migration mints a replacement device_id for the same hardware under a new identity_mode or a new secret class.

revocation_actor_class.description: Actor class that produced the most recent revocation transition. 'resolver_auto' fires for typed auto-revocations (stale_expired, capability_lost).

kind.description: Frozen capability-state kind for the optional-sync lane. Mirrors the setting-definition capability_dependency vocabulary where applicable; names the sync-only kinds (transport reachable, encryption key available, device secret present) the resolver evaluates before letting the device participate.

satisfied.description: True when the capability is currently satisfied; false locks the device out of the push / pull cycle until the capability recovers.

observed_state.description: Diagnostic label for the capability's current state (for example, 'encryption_key=missing', 'transport=unreachable', 'policy_epoch=14'). Redacted per ADR-0007.

capability_state.description: One capability-state row. Sync lanes evaluate the full set on every reconciliation tick; any unsatisfied row drives the sync session into local_authoritative_degraded.

credential_secret_class.description: ADR-0007 secret class for the device-specific key the sync transport uses to authenticate. Only the class label and the broker alias ever cross a boundary; raw key material never appears.

credential_alias_ref.description: Opaque broker alias handle the resolver resolves through the ADR-0007 secret broker. Absent when the device is operating in account_free_local without any sync transport.

last_refreshed_at.description: Monotonic stamp of the most recent successful refresh of the device secret binding. Null when the binding has never been refreshed (for example, just-registered account_free_local devices).

device_secret_binding.description: Device-secret binding. Records the class, the broker alias, and the last refresh stamp; never the raw secret.

lineage_cursor.description: Opaque monotonic cursor naming the device's position in the mutation-journal lineage. Stable across pauses; reset only on 'forgotten'. The support exporter, migration center, and conflict packet reuse this cursor to name lineage in a way that does not expose hostnames, IPs, or serials.

registered_at.description: Monotonic stamp the device first registered with the registry.

last_seen_at.description: Monotonic stamp the resolver most recently observed the device (push, pull, or heartbeat).

last_seen_source.description: Named surface that last observed the device. 'manual_export_import' fires when continuity is carried by a user-initiated export/import rather than a live transport.

export_safe_lineage.description: Export-safe lineage block. Every field is safe to quote in a support bundle; none of the fields expose raw hostnames, IPs, serials, paths, or secrets.

trust_state.description: Observed trust state at last_seen_at. Null when the device has not been seen under a concrete workspace (for example, just-registered devices before any workspace is opened).

revoked_at.description: Monotonic stamp of the most recent revocation_state transition away from 'active'. Null when the record is currently active and has never been revoked.

revoked_by_actor_class.description: Actor class that produced the most recent revocation transition. Null pairs with revocation_state=active and revoked_at=null.

capability_states.description: Observed capability-state rows. Empty when the device has no sync-specific capability dependencies (for example, an account_free_local device with no optional-sync binding).

device_secret_binding.description: Device-secret binding. Null for devices operating without a sync transport binding.

redaction_class.description: ADR-0007 redaction class applied to this record when it reaches an exportable surface (support bundle, audit stream, migration-center export). Device labels outside a private device's owner bundle resolve under 'redact_to_class_label'.

record_class_id.description: Stable handle into artifacts/governance/record_class_registry.yaml naming the retention / export posture for this record. Populated when the record is carried into a support bundle or offboarding packet.

schema_version.description: The sync device-record schema version the record conforms to. Distinct from settings_schema_version, which versions this envelope.

superseded_by_device_id.description: When revocation_reason is migration_superseded, names the device_id that replaced this record (for example, a re-registered device after an identity_mode change). Null otherwise.

note.description: Short human-readable note. Redacted per ADR-0007; never carries raw secret material, hostnames, IPs, serials, or machine-local paths.

sync_device_record.description: One sync device record. Names a device that ever participated in optional sync. The record follows the revocation lifecycle durably; pause, resume, revoke, and forget are typed state transitions, not hidden side effects.
